name: Caduceus Extended
description: The Caduceus Extended is a highly potent and amplified version of the traditional Caduceus, the staff carried by Hermes (Mercury in Roman mythology). This enhanced version is not merely a symbol of commerce, negotiation, and eloquence, but a conduit for profound magical energies. Its origin is shrouded in mystery, some say forged by Hephaestus himself using celestial metals infused with the breath of Zeus. The staff is crafted from gleaming, unbreakable orichalcum, with two intertwined serpents, each representing opposing forces like order and chaos, health and disease, life and death, meticulously detailed with scales of polished obsidian and shimmering gold. Atop the staff, instead of wings, are intertwined olive branches, symbolizing peace and prosperity, further enhancing its diplomatic power. The Caduceus Extended grants the wielder unparalleled abilities in healing, communication, and manipulation of energy. It can mend grievous wounds, instantly translate any language, and influence the minds of others with ease. However, such power comes with a responsibility to maintain balance and use it for the greater good, as its misuse could have disastrous consequences. It is a symbol of divine authority and profound understanding of the universe's intricate connections.
subtitle: Amplified Symbol of Divine Authority
origin: Forged by Hephaestus with celestial metals and Zeus's breath
currentLocation: Unknown, believed hidden in a nexus of magical energy.
associatedHeroes: None specifically, potentially those favored by Hermes

keyMyths.title: The Forging of the Caduceus Extended
keyMyths.description: Hephaestus, guided by Hermes, crafted the Caduceus Extended in his volcanic forge, using materials from the heavens. Zeus imbued it with his divine breath, granting it immense power.

keyMyths.title: The Temptation of Power
keyMyths.description: A mortal once attempted to wield the Caduceus Extended for selfish gain, but its power overwhelmed him, leading to chaos and destruction. Hermes intervened to restore balance.

sources.author: Robert Graves
sources.description: Provides general information about Hermes and the standard Caduceus.
sources.text: Provides general information about Hermes and the standard Caduceus.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Details the lineage and powers of the Greek gods, including Hephaestus.
